When did the London Marathon become all about 'celebrities?' Genuine question. It used to be about Joe public turning up in daft costumes to raise money for their charities with just the odd celeb. Now it seems to be all about 'Celebs.'

A slew of stars are donning their running shoes today as they take on the London marathon and add an athletic string to their already impressive bows. Among them were Tilly Ramsay and Cynthia Erivo, who were spotted crossing the finish line following the gruelling 26.2 mile course through the centre of the capital. Strictly star Tilly, 24, was greeted by her famous parents, Gordon and Tana Ramsay, who wrapped her in hugs and kisses once she completed the race.

In ‘64 while attending a U.S. Navy school in Bainbridge, MD, three sailors and I were pulled from classes and sent to Bethesda Hospital for 3 days…I vividly remember the blue linens on the beds. We were told the Navy was studying ESP, and participated in several tests, sessions, etc., and we did what we were told. At the conclusion of our stay, the staff told us this was all a ruse, and the study was not about ESP, but to the study of individual personalities to determine who would be best to serve together and “all get along” like the crews of nuclear submarines. Years later, I assumed this was part of developing “personal profiling” tests like the MMPI, but now wonder if the ESP study was real.

Normal woman parties during her 20s then grows up.

SERENA SMITH: In my 20s, I looked like I was living the dream. A respectable job as a civil servant, glamorous nights out, designer clothes from the likes of Jimmy Choo, Dior and Mulberry and luxury holidays to Dubai, Marbella and Portugal. My lifestyle made it seem like I had it all together, yet the reality was very different. I was earning just £17,000 a year but acting like I had a £50,000 salary rolling in - and it nearly cost me everything. I started drinking regularly aged 16. I'd binge drink my evenings away, and for the next decade or more 'living for the weekend' became my entire personality. But I admittedly refused to admit I had any problem with alcohol because I never drank at home. And that for me, meant I had 'control' over the drink, when in fact, my boozy lifestyle was leaving me in thousands of pounds worth of debt.
  • 8 comments
  • share I was a respectable civil servant but I was hiding £20,000 of debt

Nuclear is still safer than Ed Tombstone Millipede 😆

Sounding like distant thunder, the strange rumble from deep inside the building was unexpected as the floors and walls began to shake violently. Control panels inside Unit 4 of Chernobyl, or the Vladimir Ilyich Lenin Nuclear Power Plant, went berserk, flashing ominously as confused operators questioned what was happening during their safety test. The time was 1.24am, April 26, 1986. Moments later, the second explosion ripped through the reactor, spawning the worst nuclear accident in history. It unleashed 500-times the amount of radiation produced by the atomic bomb that flattened Hiroshima. Here, in scintillating detail, DEEP DIVE investigates how a botched safety drill was to blame.
  • 46 comments
  • share How the Chernobyl disaster unfolded, minute by minute

Where is the cast of Ferris Bueller's Day Off now ahead of 40th anniversary of beloved '80s classic?

The beloved John Hughes classic Ferris Bueller's Day Off is preparing to celebrate its 40th anniversary this year.The film was a box office success upon its release in theaters in June 1986, and raked in around $70 million on a budget of $5 million. It had starred Matthew Broderick, Mia Sara and Alan Ruck as teens living in the Chicago suburbs who ditch school one day for an adventure in the city, while also trying to not get caught by the Dean of Students.

Rochdale fan had him pinned down, he wrestled up an gave the fan what he had coming

A York City star appeared to strike a rival supporter during a pitch invasion during their National League title decider with Rochdale. York were dramatically promoted to the EFL after a 1-1 draw at the Crown Oil Arena, with the result helping them finish two points clear of Rochdale to secure the sole automatic promotion spot in the division. Rochdale had appeared to have won the match when Emmanuel Dieseruwe headed his side in front in the fifth minute of stoppage time.

Mel C, 52, reveals how she maintains her incredibly ripped physique and says her fellow Spice Girls 'disliked exercise' during band's heyday as 'we were all nervous about getting bulky - the aesthetic was very slim'

Mel C has shared the secrets behind her incredibly chiselled and muscular physique and she declared that 'fitness is a huge part of who I am'. The Spice Girl, 52, known to millions as Sporty Spice, has more than lived up to the nickname, maintaining her taut and gym-honed figure for decades even into her fifties. Mel recently wowed fans when she flaunted her incredibly toned physique on the cover of her upcoming new album Sweat, looking more like a gladiator than a pop star as she posed in a plunging red and navy leotard.

  • 1 video
  • share Mel C, 52, reveals how she maintains her incredibly ripped physique
